items.title: MCP generation runtime foundation — canonical registry, caller attribution, and scoped authorization
items.notes: Done 2026-07-13 (R1). One fail-fast canonical registry now owns discovery, dispatch, schema census, declared workspace scope, and per-tool error compatibility for all 13 existing families / 61 tools. All existing registered handlers retain legacy text behavior; registry-owned unknown/auth rejections are generic and non-reflective. Future tools can opt into an end-to-end sanitized/bounded json_v1 envelope, including handler-returned and thrown failures. Authenticated MCP execution carries an immutable bounded request/tool/workspace/key snapshot through explicit handler context plus a concurrency-safe compatibility bridge for existing activity writers. Full admin activity retains key attribution; client reads and shared broadcasts remove it, and admin-only key lifecycle events emit opaque invalidations. Request correlation is server-owned: the UUID used by HTTP logs/responses and durable attribution is generated internally, every caller X-Request-ID is ignored, and registry rejections exclude raw unknown tool/workspace values. Scoped auth fails closed without key ID/label, conflicting aliases remain rejected, and production dispatch census pins exact family handlers plus pre-dispatch handled-name manifests. No migration, job, AI call, product tool, endpoint, flag activation, lifecycle change, or UI ships here. Future durable generation runs must persist the explicit context snapshot rather than rely on ambient request state. Closes mcp-key-label-attribution rather than duplicating it.

items.title: Content matrix to audited page-set generation — explicit cells, deterministic preflight, durable batch, and MCP
items.notes: Replace the ambiguous keyword-only template bridge with explicit matrix/cell identity and monotonic matrix-definition/template/per-cell revisions; generation projection for one cell cannot stale siblings from the same preview. Resolve the full page block manifest, version-conditionally upgrade legacy templates, reject unsupported page types, and persist normalized run/item/attempt plus cell-evidence ledgers before one-cell brief→post closure. Typed evidence resolution requires durable source refs and fresh preview/audit; deleting placeholder text never clears a requirement. Add deterministic + typed persona/SEO/AEO/CTA item audit, one total bounded revision, post-item set audit, and a resumable cost-bounded matrix job exposed through paged MCP tools. Wire the inert Generate Brief(s) UI only after the domain/MCP path is truthful. Hard dependencies: genq-content-generation-integrity C2 and genq-content-context-v2 C3. Automatic generation stops at ready_for_human_review; explicit approval may advance to ready_to_publish; never auto-approve/send/publish. Final phase makes service/location or collection-blueprint inputs create/link a validated matrix. || [P0 CONTRACTS 2026-07-13] Shared matrix source revisions, structural-vs-ready targets, factual-source exclusions, typed evidence/placeholder policy, exact system block census, exact brief/post CAS, cell-addressed resolution, a non-empty preview-fingerprinted paid selection, operator-authorized replacement, truthful audit/run/item status, a shared 0|1 automatic-revision cap, and the reserved OFF content-matrix-generation flag are implemented. || [M0 FOUNDATION 2026-07-13] Matrix/template/per-cell CAS, deterministic structural rendering and legacy-template upgrade acceptance, bounded list/get/resolve/upgrade MCP tools, complete workspace URL collision census, and normalized run/item/attempt/evidence ledgers are implemented. Cell snapshot cursors conflict on cell-only edits; wholesale cell PUTs are rejected. Direct cell mutations use target-cell CAS and preserve history/side effects; matrix-definition regeneration uses matrix CAS and intentionally clears stale lifecycle/artifact state. The repository accepts only strict already-previewed selections and retains secret-free provenance/source identity. No paid generation, preview/start route, AI call, artifact write, approval, send, or publish exists yet; M1-M4 remain pending.

items.title: Brand intake to reviewed brand suite — durable evidence, voice finalization, generation/audit, MCP, and Inbox
items.source: User-directed Brand & Web Onboarding / IE pre-seed upgrade, 2026-07-13
items.notes: Persist typed immutable intake revisions and make compatibility projection idempotent. Full-suite generation first bootstraps only a provisional voice foundation from intake/authentic samples, then pauses with no running job; an operator must select authentic anchors and explicitly finalize that exact voice version before MCP resume can generate dependent identity/messaging/audience pieces. Exhaustive atomic/preset policies make voice_foundation the sole bootstrap deliverable and full_brand_system the sole bootstrap-then-resume preset; every dependent direct target/other bundle requires the exact finalized voice version. Typed evidence resolution creates a superseding intake revision, never clears from text deletion. Use named structured AI operations, CAS/provenance, hard budgets, bounded audit/revision, and naming as a creative proposal with no clearance claims. Reuse unified ClientDeliverable for grouped per-item review with honest partial state; client approval never finalizes voice. Project approved client-safe brand summary only. || [P0 CONTRACTS 2026-07-13] Typed immutable intake, singular atomic/full-suite initial-resume policy, authentic voice-sample origins, operator anchor selection/finalization, immutable approved-identity refs, human review decisions, client-safe summary, truthful bounded audit/run/item states, dormant naming vocabulary/guardrails, and the reserved OFF brand-deliverable-generation flag are implemented. Legacy paid generate and refine boundaries both reject naming before usage/AI; no reviewed naming or brand batch route is opened. || [B0 DURABLE INTAKE 2026-07-13] The existing client questionnaire, authenticated admin read/evidence mutation, and two dedicated MCP json_v1 tools now share one immutable revision service. Exact retries are actor/source-bound; evidence resolution creates or reuses a typed superseding revision with revision/idempotency checks; compatibility projection preserves calibrated voice authority and explicitly owned competitor provenance; post-commit activity, workspace broadcast, and intelligence invalidation happen once. Raw intake remains absent from client/public serializers, and the dead Brandscript label parser is retired. No AI call, brand-generation run, Inbox delivery, client approval, or voice finalization ships here, so B1-B3 remain pending. || [B1 FINALIZED VOICE 2026-07-13] Voice profiles now have monotonic revisions and immutable operator-finalized versions that freeze exact DNA, substantive guardrails, modifiers, authentic anchor content/evidence, and calibration selections into an immutable versioned snapshot. Generic edits and sample mutations reopen calibrated profiles; readiness distinguishes missing/finalized/stale authority, while legacy calibrated rows remain prompt-compatible but cannot unlock B2/M1 generation. Human HTTP finalization stays available; MCP finalization consumes a short-lived one-time operator authorization whose plaintext token is never stored, records the MCP key only as private execution provenance, and replays without phantom side effects. Site-scraped drafts no longer claim calibration, legacy voice deliverables cannot compete with calibrated Layer 2, and the brand intelligence slice carries readiness/revision/version. No paid generation, brand suite, Inbox delivery, client approval, or publication ships here, so B2-B3 remain pending. || [B2 GROUNDED BRAND GENERATION 2026-07-13] Exact immutable intake/voice/approved-brand snapshots now drive normalized, budget-bounded run/item/command/attempt ledgers through named generate/refine/audit operations. Full-suite start produces and audits only a structured provisional foundation, ends its job at the human voice-finalization gate, and resume creates the 18 durable dependents only from a later exact finalized voice. Candidate barriers, deterministic/model checkpoints, one bounded revision, final-only CAS, item-only attention/blocked output, conflict preservation, restart reconciliation, and summary-only jobs keep partial outcomes truthful. Four snake-case json_v1 MCP tools plus authenticated HTTP adapters start/read/resume/revise without exposing prompts, evidence bodies, idempotency keys, or MCP key identity. || [B3 GROUPED CLIENT REVIEW 2026-07-13] Exact B2 runs now send through the canonical ClientDeliverable/Inbox spine as separate advisory-foundation or durable-suite bundles. Per-item decisions atomically CAS source status, generation ledger/counts, and mirror state; opaque review tokens bind decisions to exactly what the client saw across stable-ID resends. Exact pending retries do not re-notify, legitimate revisions preserve terminal siblings, partial bundles may return to awaiting review, and team notifications/activity/workspace invalidations remain isolated from one another after commit. Public reads, mutation receipts, activity, and shared brand-identity events explicitly remove run/source/revision/actor lineage. The client Inbox supports independent approve/change requests, conflict/unknown-outcome recovery, accessible focus, safe prose rendering, and honest grouped status; the Brand tab reads only approved deliverables and safe voice summary with live invalidation. MCP send_to_client accepts one exact brand-generation target and never auto-approves/finalizes/publishes. B0-B3 are complete; the reserved rollout flag remains default OFF pending staged activation.

items.title: Resumable MCP onboarding orchestration — intake → brand review → approved voice/identity → matrix pages
items.notes: After the independent brand and matrix lanes are staging-verified, add the shared BrandContentOnboardingRun lifecycle, durable versioned record, and MCP start/get/resume surface. The workflow pauses at voice review/finalization, operator review, client review, content authorization, and a distinct page-review gate after content generation; no generic job stays open and no human gate is bypassed. It freezes the approved brand snapshot for the page run. Automatic output stops at ready_for_human_review; every selected page needs version-safe review-only approval that satisfies export/publish preconditions but never invokes auto-publish/CMS, even when configured. Only then may awaiting_content_review advance to ready_to_publish; publication remains separate. Requires both underlying rollout flags rather than a composite flag. || [P0 CONTRACTS 2026-07-13] The shared run/status/gate vocabulary and discriminated durable gate evidence now exist, including non-empty matrix selection, operator/client content authorization proof, and page approval refs frozen to matrix run/item/cell and post revisions with a human approver. Persistence, transitions, and MCP orchestration remain pending O1 after both underlying lanes validate.

items.title: AI execution governance — operation cache policy, provider-neutral run telemetry, and provenance contracts
items.notes: Done 2026-07-13. Named-operation none/inflight/ttl policy now governs dispatcher-backed OpenAI and Anthropic text calls; signal-bearing calls remain uncached and cancellation-isolated. Execution metadata, provider attempts, token usage, operation traces, cache outcomes, origin-run reuse, and real Claude-to-GPT fallback chains share truthful correlation IDs without storing prompts. Cache statistics use cumulative events and real time windows, live pending calls are never age-evicted into duplicate provider work, and the typed GenerationProvenance contract is ready for durable consumers. The unused typed Anthropic tool helper remains explicitly cache-none and outside dispatcher governance until a production caller is introduced.

items.title: Keyword evidence integrity — preserve stored intent, CPC, source, and provenance through scoring
items.notes: Done 2026-07-13. Stored search intent now survives the lite page adapter into real KCC routes. CPC, intent, membership source, and field-level evidence provenance survive canonical/fallback pools, incremental competitor cache, assignment, every enrichment writer, page/content-gap persistence, sanitizer replacement, and the recommendation Opportunity Value path; unusable CPC remains unknown instead of authoritative zero. Keyword strategy generation now stores an exact effective-input fingerprint plus provider-neutral run provenance, uses a monotonic revision, and commits with transactional CAS so every audited feedback/vote/managed-set/tracked-keyword/business-priority/operator edit wins without repeating provider work. Internal provenance is omitted from workspace/public serialization. Migrations 179-180.

items.title: Content generation integrity — truthful completion, strict artifacts, edit-safe saves, and provenance
items.notes: Wave 2-3. Phase A (C1) implemented 2026-07-13: strict initial brief/copy schemas are separate from partial updates; copy generation enforces the exact planned-section census with one bounded repair; useful visible-text partial posts persist as needs_attention with typed sanitized diagnostics and failure-only job/activity semantics; markup-only or unusable output fails; full and single-section regeneration preserve the exact prior artifact on failure; invalid unifier candidates are rejected atomically so every pre-unification field survives; incomplete artifacts are blocked from client send, export, Webflow publish, intelligence, and MCP creation; MCP post saves enforce canonical visible content, contiguous indexes, and the prepared brief census; rebuilt admin surfaces expose amber repair actions/diagnostics; public reads omit internal diagnostics. Phase B (C2) remains pending: generation revisions, typed durable provenance, resource-scoped idempotency, and version-conditional saves for posts, briefs, and copy sections so concurrent operator edits always win. Depends on genq-ai-execution-governance.

items.title: Recommendation integrity — stale-write protection, stable identity, retirement, and mirror repair
items.notes: S1 / Phase A complete 2026-07-13: recommendation sets now carry a monotonic generation revision plus internal run provenance, generation commits transactionally with CAS, and one conflict re-finalizes cloned already-generated candidates against the latest lifecycle state without repeating provider work; a second conflict fails safely. Concurrent sends, approvals, dismissals, strikes, and operator-minted recommendations win, and finalized counts drive success logs/events. Status remains pending for S2 / Phase B: prevent resolved-signal reminting, stabilize content-gap/issue identity, make suppression merge-key-local, preserve evidence priority caps, and repair recommendation/deliverable divergence. S2 must update and reuse strategy-divergence-sweep for bounded observation/repair; never enable strategy-trust-ladder-autosend.

items.title: Keyword Command Center read performance — first-paint hydration and a true skinny projection
items.notes: Complete 2026-07-13. First paint now uses /initial exactly once to seed independent canonical summary and row caches; subsequent interactions use /rows without re-running full hydration. KCC reads use a context-owned skinny projection instead of the full strategy assembler, rank freshness is explicit, local-candidate counts preserve market-identity parity, and production query-count and p95 budgets are registered and pinned with real SQL execution instrumentation. This closes the remaining keyword-hub-wave4 read-path work.

items.notes: Wave 2-3, amended 2026-07-13 after the K3 persisted-identity audit. K3a is complete: it documents the v1 identity census and collision/alias contract. K3b is complete in PR scope: versioned v1/v2 helpers, additive dual-read/dual-write compatibility across tracked/site/feedback/vote/SERP/local/cache seams, and a dry-run-default collision-safe TypeScript backfill ship while canonical behavior remains v1. The required staging dry-run, apply, and zero-write second apply are an operational gate after deploy, not claimed here. K3c remains pending and may switch the canonical helper to NFKC + Unicode letters/numbers only after that staging report verifies zero orphaned feedback/evidence. Raw provider/display values never change; unrecoverable v1-only feedback/vote/SERP identities remain readable aliases. K4 then guarantees requested/voted inclusion plus source/intent/market diversity behind the new per-workspace keyword-synthesis-candidate-recall flag and requires deterministic fixtures plus a staging canary.

items.title: Content generation context v2 — budgeted evidence projection and exact-once voice authority
items.notes: Wave 4 after content integrity. Build one brief-relevant budgeted context, remove repeated page-map injection, preserve exact-once voice/guardrail authority, and wrap scraped evidence as untrusted with freshness metadata. Output semantics are staged behind per-workspace content-generation-context-v2 and promoted only with quality parity plus measured token/latency improvement.

items.title: Ordered intelligence convergence — recompute, regenerate recommendations, then broadcast freshness
items.notes: Wave 4 after recommendation integrity. Replace independently scheduled follow-ons with mutation → intelligence recompute → debounced recommendation regeneration → freshness broadcast. Reuse current job dedupe/rate limits and do not resurrect retired signal-auto-recompute. Canonical successor that absorbs and closes strategy-redesign-phase-5c-auto-recompute.

items.title: Runtime AI quality governance — observed budgets and critical pipeline coverage

Join jobs, AI/provider attempts, and durable artifacts by run ID; compare observed p50/p95, tokens, cost, calls, and queries to budgets; add reliability/quality pipelines for keyword strategy, recommendations, Strategy POV, and content; consume the AI stats window/cache-rate correction owned by genq-ai-execution-governance. Prerequisite for client-facing outcome confidence and conversion calibration rollout.

items.title: GBP follow-on — update existing Google review replies
items.notes: Phase 2C intentionally keeps existing Google replies read-only. Add an explicit update workflow only after reply publishing has been validated on staging: load current Google/local reply state, draft a replacement, require client/admin reapproval, preserve activity/history, publish through Google's updateReply endpoint, and prevent silent overwrites or publish without fresh approval.

items.title: GBP follow-on — bulk review response drafting queue

Must use the background job platform, cost/rate caps, per-review draft validation, and admin review before any send or publish. No auto-send, no auto-publish, and raw review text remains limited to the per-review drafting operation.

items.title: GBP follow-on — scheduled review auto-responder approval loop
items.notes: Turn new synced reviews into scheduled or event-triggered draft responses with explicit client/admin approval before publishing. Must be opt-in per workspace/client, include notification cadence and escalation rules, throttle generation, route low-rating reviews for human attention, and preserve the no-auto-publish guarantee.

items.title: GBP follow-on — review response effectiveness reporting
items.notes: Add reporting for unanswered review aging, response SLA, published replies, failed publishes, low-rating handling, and client approval turnaround. items.title: P0 — inbox decision tools (update approval item / respond to client action)
items.notes: Done. Two MCP tools in server/mcp/tools/clients.ts (alongside get_pending_work; auto-routed via clientTools.some): (1) respond_to_client_action — routes through the admin service updateAdminClientAction (transition guard + activity + broadcast + insight/outcome feedback loop); status approved/changes_requested/completed/archived/pending. (2) respond_to_approval_item — DECLINE-ONLY by owner decision: routes through respondToApprovalBatchItem with status hardcoded to rejected (request changes) + actor {name:MCP agent} (honest attribution, not Client). An agent CANNOT approve approval items on the client behalf (approval is the client review decision + sends approved team emails). Schemas respondToClientActionInputSchema/respondToApprovalItemInputSchema (no status field on the approval one). Routes through domain services that already broadcast (mcp-action-must-broadcast mutationFnRe does not match them — no double broadcast). Test tests/unit/mcp-inbox-tools.test.ts (10) incl. the decline-only + honest-actor contract. circular-deps 0/0. Closes the read-only inbox loop (client-action half fully; approval half decline-only).

items.title: P0 — rate-limit /mcp + real paid-call budget covering the two paid jobs
items.notes: Done. (1) Rate-limit /mcp: new mcpLimiter=rateLimit(60s,120) [per-path, NOT global — global would share globalPublicLimiter's  bucket and cross-contaminate /mcp with /api/public/] in server/middleware.ts, applied via a top-level req.path===/mcp check (mirrors publicWriteLimiter) so the bucket key is  (/mcp was unthrottled — the 3 public limiters only cover /api/public/). 120 req/min/IP — generous for a normal agent workflow, bounds a runaway loop / leaked-key abuser; 429 + Retry-After on exceed. (2) Paid-call counting parity: start_keyword_strategy_generation + start_local_seo_refresh now call recordPaidCall(1) and surface the warning (previously only research_keywords counted); both tool descriptions tagged [Paid API]. Tests: mcp-rate-limit.test.ts (120 allowed / 429 after) + extended mcp-tools-job-actions.test.ts (paid jobs count, seo_audit does not). DEFERRED: hard paid-call ENFORCEMENT (circuit breaker that refuses calls over a budget) is intentionally NOT here — the counter is a per-instance module-global let, so real per-workspace durable budgets belong with the P1 durable-handle-store item (mcp-durable-handle-store). The rate limiter is the request-level circuit breaker; this completes the informational paid-call signal.

items.title: P1 — publish_post + advance_content_status (close the content loop)
items.notes: Done (closes the content loop — the third stateless loop after analytics + inbox). Two tools in server/mcp/tools/content-actions.ts (auto-routed via the if-name dispatch): (1) advance_content_status(workspace_id, request_id, status, internal_note?) routes through updateContentRequest (validates the transition) + addActivity(content_updated, source mcp-chat) + broadcast(CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE). status RESTRICTED to in_progress | delivered (operator-workflow only) — client_review/post_review email the client (go through send_to_client), approved/changes_requested are the client's portal decisions, published is publish_post, declined is a separate dismissal. (2) publish_post(workspace_id, post_id, generate_image?) wraps publishPostToWebflow (the shared service that owns the field map + broadcast CONTENT_PUBLISHED + activity + outcome) — APPROVED-ONLY by owner decision: refuses unless post.status==='approved' (an agent can never push an un-reviewed draft to the client's LIVE site, even though the service itself permits draft/review admin-override). Extended PublishPostToWebflowOptions.activitySource union with 'mcp-chat' for attribution (behaves like 'manual'). Schemas advanceContentStatusInputSchema/publishPostInputSchema (with .describe() per the P0 contract test). Tests extended in tests/unit/mcp-tools-content.test.ts incl. the approved-only refusal across draft/review/generating/error. circular-deps 0/0. Adversarial review pending.

items.title: Voice-sample dedup on deliverable edit/re-approval (provenance-aware)
items.notes: setDeliverableStatus auto-creates a voice_sample (source 'identity_approved', 500-char snapshot, no link back to the deliverable) when a tagline/elevator_pitch/tone_examples deliverable is approved. Editing (now cheap via update_brand_deliverable) resets to draft; re-approving creates a FRESH sample rather than updating the prior one → stale/near-dup samples can accumulate and dilute voice calibration. DECISION 2026-06-29: LEAVE AS-IS — the sample/deliverable decoupling is deliberate (samples are independently curatable; revert-to-draft intentionally doesn't delete the sample), frequency is low, impact mild, and a proper fix fights that design. REVISIT TRIGGER: agent-driven edits churn these 3 types frequently and the VoiceTab fills with near-dup samples. Then: add source_deliverable_id to voice_samples + non-destructive 'update-if-unedited' logic (don't clobber curator edits). Cheap interim option: idempotency guard skipping an auto-sample when an identical-content 'identity_approved' sample already exists. Owner may remove. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OBSOLETE-CLOSE with no implementation. Independent sample curation and low observed churn outweigh provenance-linked dedup complexity. Reopen only with evidence of near-duplicate sample accumulation.

items.title: API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger spec)
items.source: Market prep analysis Mar 2026; revised May 2026 post-review
items.notes: 91 route files in server/routes/ with no formal API spec. Triggers to pull forward: (a) first external API consumer signed (paid agency white-label, Zapier, contractor onboarding), (b) committing to migrate response validation to Zod for other reasons (OpenAPI becomes a free byproduct), (c) hitting 'I don't know what this endpoint returns' friction during builds. Implementation: use @asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi — NOT tsoa (requires controller-class refactor incompatible with Express+middleware style) or swagger-jsdoc (hand-maintained doc blocks rot immediately). Prerequisites before start: (1) response schemas must be migrated from shared/types/ TS interfaces to Zod (currently only request schemas are Zod via validate() middleware) — this is the bulk of the work, not the spec emission, (2) per-route security tagging for the dual auth model (HMAC x-auth-token admin vs JWT client vs public token), (3) pr-check rule asserting every Express route has a registered OpenAPI entry to prevent drift (zod-to-openapi does NOT introspect the route table — registration is opt-in per route). Out of scope: WebSocket broadcasts (would need separate AsyncAPI spec). Deliverables: registered request+response schemas per route, /api/docs Swagger UI gated to authenticated admins only, generated spec in CI artifact, drift-detection test. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but correctly framed as a TRIGGERED/gated item, not an active backlog item. The note's gating triggers (first external API consumer, Zod-response-migration done for other reasons, or build-time 'what does this return' friction) are all unrealized for a solo-founder platform with no external API consumers. Do not pull forward until a trigger fires. The implementation guidance (zod-to-openapi, dual-auth security tagging, drift-detection pr-check rule) is still architecturally sound for the current Express+middleware style. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: MCP is the preferred guarded external action surface. Re-enter OpenAPI work only for a signed REST consumer or a deliberate response-schema migration.

sprints.name: Intelligence Quality Consolidation — Recommendations & AI Content
sprints.rationale: Close the root quality gaps in platform insights and AI content generation by joining mature subsystems through the existing intelligence conventions instead of hand-rolling context, scoring, narrative, and evidence handling per feature. The target architecture is: workspace data -> intelligence slices -> shared generation context -> AI operation contract -> deterministic recommendation/story output -> tracked action/outcome -> future intelligence. The remaining keyword work extends this consolidation into a full keyword operating loop: provider source data -> shared keyword intelligence -> strategy/recommendation judgment -> rank-tracking propagation -> content/action follow-through -> outcome feedback.

items.title: Per-market keyword relevance — keyword↔market assignment model
items.notes: Today the refresh planner pairs every selected keyword with every active market — `dentist at houston` gets queried against Austin's market and surfaces Houston competitors as 'top local results' in the Austin context. Confirmed on Swish staging refresh 2026-05-21 (55 of 200 snapshots = 27.5% cross-market noise). Fix is non-trivial because it requires a keyword↔market assignment model. (1) New `keyword_markets` join table mapping which keywords should be checked against which markets. (2) Default: keyword applies to all active markets (matches current implicit behavior, no migration of existing data needed). (3) Override path: admin pins a keyword to specific market(s). (4) Auto-inference: if keyword text contains an active market's city/region name, default assignment to that market only. (5) Iterator + refresh planner respect the assignment. (6) UI: per-market detail blocks in keyword drawer instead of single block, with pin/unpin controls. (7) **Per-market volume/clicks/impressions reporting**: the keyword detail surface should show separate volume, GSC clicks, GSC impressions, and CTR per active market the keyword applies to — today these are workspace-global and don't distinguish between Austin and Houston performance for the same keyword. Requires per-market metric storage AND geo-targeted DataForSEO volume queries (see intel-quality-keyword-volume-geo-targeting). Deferred to P2 because no current client uses multi-market in production — Swish is the only multi-market workspace and is internal. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-confirm priority. The item is still framed correctly for the current architecture (the planner pairing it describes is verbatim what exists). The deferral rationale also still holds: per the notes, Swish is the only multi-market workspace and it's internal — so the 27.5% cross-market noise has zero production client impact today. Recommend keeping P2/deferred and gating activation on the FIRST paying multi-market client rather than building speculatively. When built, sub-points 1-5 (join table, default-all, pin override, city-name auto-inference, planner respect) are the load-bearing core; sub-points 6-7 (per-market UI + per-market metrics) depend on the also-pending geo-targeted volume item and should be a follow-up phase. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: candidate market tagging ships, but the assignment model should wait for a paying multi-market client and demonstrated cross-product noise.

items.title: Per-workspace activity_log retention (replace global 500-row cap)
items.notes: AUDIT high/small. server/activity-log.ts:166 MAX_ENTRIES=500 is GLOBAL; pruneOldest (lines 265-269) deletes oldest across ALL workspaces. Fix: per-workspace cap or time cutoff (created_at < now-180d); move prune to a schedule, not synchronously on every insert (231 call sites hit a COUNT(*)+prune in a hot path). || DONE 2026-06-25: replaced the global activity_log cap with scheduled per-workspace retention (`MAX_ENTRIES_PER_WORKSPACE`) run through the existing daily data-retention cycle, so high-volume workspaces cannot evict quiet workspace feeds and `addActivity()` no longer runs count/prune work in the hot insert path. items.title: SQLite + read-path tuning (PRAGMAs, blob reads, discovered_queries retention, slice parallelism)
items.notes: AUDIT low-med (bundle). (a) discovered_queries (largest table, 7,107 rows) has NO prune anywhere (unlike rank_snapshots/roi_snapshots which prune by date); getLostVisibilityKeys builds an unbounded in-memory Set — add a retention policy + LIMIT. (b) Default SQLite PRAGMAs leave headroom: add synchronous=NORMAL (safe under WAL) + larger cache (currently 2MB) in db/index.ts. (c) performance_snapshots.result GSC blobs (~160KB) parsed wholesale per read — same pattern as recommendation_sets, less acute (point reads). (d) Intelligence slices assembled sequentially (no Promise.all) — parallelize when slice count/cost grows. Progress 2026-06-26: added WAL-safe synchronous=NORMAL and cache_size=-20000 PRAGMAs; capped lost-visibility row/key read models to the top 200 rows while keeping count semantics uncapped; added discovered_queries pruning from both the rank scheduler and global data-retention cron with one-year retention while preserving the top 200 lost-visibility rows. DONE 2026-06-26: parallelized workspace intelligence slice assembly while preserving requested-order assignment and per-slice failure isolation; moved competitor performance snapshot list/latest selection onto SQLite JSON projection so list views and latest-candidate matching do not parse every stored result blob. Deferred recommendation captured as simp-delta-0626-pagespeed-snapshot-projection: PageSpeed/admin-chat/site-health/page-profile still need a dedicated summary/page-score projection rather than full result blob reads.

items.notes: New server/cron-registry.ts: CRON_METADATA (Record<CronId, {label; module; intervalMs; description; stopHook; start; stop; exemptReason?}>) mirroring shared/types/background-jobs.ts's BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA shape. Lazy construction — the module's top-level scope never calls start()/stop(); only startAllRegisteredCrons()/stopAllRegisteredCrons() (called from server/startup.ts and server/index.ts gracefulShutdown respectively) invoke lifecycle hooks. Registers all 19 boot-wired scheduler subsystems (email-throttle, scheduled-audits, approval-reminders, monthly-reports, backup, trial-reminders, churn-signals, anomaly-detection, outcome-crons, data-retention, intelligence-cache-refresh, competitor-monitoring, insight-recompute, rank-tracking, ga4-conversion-snapshot, webflow-form-poller, briefing-cron, strategy-issue-cron, return-hook-cron) plus 3 known module-level timers as documented stopHook:false exemptions (server/middleware.ts rate-limit + login-lockout cleanup, server/ai-deduplication.ts dedup cache cleanup — unconditional import-time timers with no existing stop export; restructuring deferred, not silently dropped) and the MCP TTL sweeper (server/mcp/handles.ts, already NODE_ENV=test guarded) similarly exempt. Closed all 9 stop-hook gaps the R10 inventory found: added stopBackupScheduler (backup.ts had none) and wired 4 previously-orphaned stop exports (ga4-conversion-snapshot, webflow-form-poller, strategy-issue-cron, return-hook-cron) into gracefulShutdown via the single stopAllRegisteredCrons() call — replacing the old hand-listed 14-call block. server/startup.ts now delegates to startAllRegisteredCrons() instead of 19 hand-listed imports (initEmailQueue + clearTestModeCustomerIds remain hand-called — one-shot, not recurring crons). New tests/unit/cron-registry.test.ts (metadata completeness, exemptReason required when stopHook:false, lazy-construction guard, start/stop-all helpers) and tests/contract/cron-registry-census.test.ts (anti-drift census: every boot-wired module + known module-level timer has a registry entry; gracefulShutdown/startSchedulers wired through the registry helpers). Simplified tests/unit/startup.test.ts from mocking 15-of-20 scheduler imports (the historical hazard — 5 unmocked real modules could start real timers inside vitest) down to mocking the 3 modules startup.ts now actually imports (email.js, stripe.js, cron-registry.js). Deferred per owner decision: throttledUntil-to-cron conversion DROPPED (not implemented); strategy-issue-cron.ts and outcome-crons.ts are registered as metadata entries but their internal construction/execution-surface adoption (createIntervalCron) is NOT refactored — deferred to a follow-up after B6 (measured R4×R10 file collision); the 16 hand-rolled interval modules are not migrated onto the shared createIntervalCron primitive — that consolidation is R10-PR2. pr-check rule ('new setInterval must register in CRON_METADATA') evaluated and explicitly DEFERRED — documented in docs/rules/background-generation.md's new §Cron Registry: a regex/customCheck rule cannot reliably distinguish a boot-wired module-level scheduler from a request-scoped setInterval (e.g. the SSE keepalive in server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts) without real scope analysis. Honest scope of the census contract test: it catches an already-inventoried scheduler LOSING its CRON_METADATA entry, cron-registry.ts dropping an inventory import, gracefulShutdown/startSchedulers no longer routing through the registry helpers, and startup.ts bypassing the registry via a direct scheduler import — but it does NOT auto-detect a brand-new scheduler absent from its hardcoded inventory (it iterates that list, not startup.ts's live imports), so adding a new scheduler is a manual two-step: register in cron-registry.ts AND extend the census inventory. Auto-detecting an un-inventoried new scheduler is what the deferred pr-check rule would add. The R10 inventory's claimed background-jobs-lifecycle.test.ts gap (missing 409 non-cancellable HTTP pin) was found to already exist at tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts:89 — not duplicated. docs/rules/background-generation.md + CLAUDE.md pointer updated same-commit. Verified: npm run typecheck clean, npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts zero errors, targeted vitest (cron-registry.test.ts 7 + cron-registry-census.test.ts 7 + startup.test.ts 1) 15/15 green.

items.title: R10-PR2 — Cancel-gate pin + system-job class (Lane I final ticket)
items.notes: Re-verified the ratified 'job-cancel route must consult BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA[type].cancellable' deliverable: ALREADY implemented at HEAD (server/routes/jobs.ts:193 getJobCancellationError, landed 914b68f19) with an existing HTTP 409 integration test at tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts:89 — no re-implementation, no duplicate test added. Real remaining work: system-job class. Added BackgroundJobClass ('user'|'system') + class field to every BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA entry + isSystemJobType(type) predicate in shared/types/background-jobs.ts, keyed by job TYPE (not a DB column — zero migrations, jobs table untouched, works retroactively on already-persisted rows). INTELLIGENCE_RECOMPUTE is the sole 'system' entry; all other 37 types are 'user'. It is created exclusively through server/intelligence-recompute-job.ts's enqueueIntelligenceRecompute (flag-gated behind signal-auto-recompute, hasActiveJob-deduped), called from THREE producer paths so removing any single caller does NOT retire the type: (1) server/insight-recompute-cron.ts (daily activity-gated cron), (2) server/rank-tracking-scheduler.ts (daily rank-tracking cron, refreshing signals after new rank snapshots land), (3) server/keyword-strategy-follow-ons.ts (on-mutation follow-on after a strategy/content update settles — not a cron). Because class is a property of the job TYPE, all three paths are gated identically; which caller enqueued it is irrelevant to client visibility. Wired isSystemJobType as a second, independent gate (always wins on top of the existing CLIENT_VISIBLE_JOB_TYPES allow-list, so a type mistakenly allow-listed in future still can't leak) into both client-visibility surfaces: server/routes/jobs.ts isClientVisibleJob() (GET /api/public/jobs, feeds AgencyWorkFeed.tsx via useClientWorkFeed.ts) and server/websocket.ts resolveJobDelivery() (unauthenticated WS delivery path for JOB_CREATED/JOB_UPDATED). Admin surfaces (GET /api/jobs, authenticated WS path, NotificationBell.tsx, useBackgroundTasks.tsx) were already unfiltered by type and required no change — 'admin shows everything' was already true by construction. No WS payload/schema change (class is derived client-side from job.type via the shared metadata table, exactly like cancellable already is), so no contract-test/handler updates were needed beyond the new pinning assertions themselves — JOB_CREATED/JOB_UPDATED stay in ws-invalidation-coverage's LOCAL_ONLY_EVENTS unchanged. TDD: extended tests/unit/background-jobs.test.ts + tests/unit/shared-types-validation.test.ts (isSystemJobType classification + safe unknown-type default), tests/unit/websocket-job-delivery.test.ts (system job reaches admin, blocked from unauthenticated client even hypothetically allow-listed), tests/integration/agency-work-feed.test.ts (system job absent from GET /api/public/jobs, present via GET /api/jobs). docs/rules/background-generation.md new §System Job Class documents the contract + narrowest-filter-point rationale. Verified: npm run typecheck clean, npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts zero errors, targeted vitest 15 files / 418 tests green (background-jobs, shared-types-validation, websocket-job-delivery, agency-work-feed, jobs-routes, background-jobs-lifecycle, jobs-cancellation, mcp-tools-job-actions, mcp-tools-jobs, background-job-coverage-contract, background-job-task-panel-wiring, mcp-tool-job-name-lockstep, useBackgroundTasks.realtime, ws-invalidation-coverage, websocket-event-shapes). Lane I (R10) complete.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Design cleanup — operator + client surface hierarchy & system consolidation
sprints.rationale: From the 2026-06-30 UX reviews of three surfaces judged against the design system primitives and the four laws of color: WorkspaceOverview (agency Command Center), WorkspaceHome (single-workspace operator home), and TheIssueClientPage (the verdict-first client money surface, flag the-issue-client-spine). Shared root cause on the operator screens: everything is surfaced at equal weight, so the eye has nowhere to land. The client surface is already strong — its items are polish + lifting recurring patterns into shared components. All fixes reuse existing primitives. Full findings index: design-system/guidelines/ux-findings-index.html. Per-screen artifacts: ux-review-command-center.html, ux-review-workspace-home.html, ux-review-client-issue.html. Item id prefixes map to screen: cc-=Command Center, wh-=Workspace Home, issue-=The Issue client, x-=cross-cutting. Canonical spec + per-item files/dependsOn/acceptanceCriteria/verify: docs/design-cleanup/design-cleanup-sprint.json. Working agreement: docs/design-cleanup/design-cleanup-working-agreement.md. Build plan: docs/design-cleanup/design-cleanup-sprint-brief.html.

items.title: Command Center header — collapse 5 colored buttons to one neutral cluster + one primary
items.notes: WorkspaceOverview header renders Prospect/Roadmap/AI Usage/Revenue/Features as five equal secondary buttons, three individually colored amber/emerald/teal — spending the status palette on navigation (teal 'Features' reads as the primary CTA, amber 'AI Usage' reads as a warning). Fix: demote the five to one neutral secondary cluster or an overflow 'More' menu, promote the actual entry action (new/onboard workspace) to the single mint primary, and strip amber/emerald/teal off chrome. Reserve those hues for status only. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Command Center — promote Needs Attention to the top with real presence
items.notes: Needs Attention is the prioritized, deep-linked worklist — the reason operators open the screen — but it sits mid-page at default weight below the header. Fix: make it the first block under PageHeader; add a count in the title ('Needs Attention · 6'); add a subtle red/amber left-accent when a P1 (churn/new request) exists; keep the chevron always visible so rows read as actionable. Component already exists (SectionCard + ClickableRow); this is ordering + emphasis only. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Command Center — one headline metric, drop stats duplicated by Needs Attention
items.notes: Global stat row shows six equal StatCards; nothing leads, and New Requests / Approvals / Content duplicate the Needs Attention list directly above. Fix: render one headline (Hours Saved — the value story) at size=hero spanning two columns, keep only at-a-glance trend metrics (Avg Health, Active, Content), and drop the actionable counts that already live in Needs Attention. Reduces the row from 6 to ~4 with a clear lead. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Command Center — workspace rows lead with score + single rollup, details on demand
items.notes: Each workspace row packs five metric columns (Health/Requests/Approvals/Content/SEO Status) with inline colored micro-text in every cell; at 3+ workspaces it becomes a wall of multicolored numbers and color loses meaning. Fix: lead each row with the ranking number (health score, in band color) + a single 'N need you' rollup pill; move the five-column breakdown into the row's hover/expanded state or the workspace detail. Color only on the rollup; neutral text elsewhere. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Color-law sweep — stop using mint (text-accent-brand) on static data
items.notes: Static counts are rendered in text-accent-brand (mint) across the overview ('3 active', '5 delivered', approvals count). Mint is the action color; on static numbers it makes everything read as clickable and dilutes real CTAs. Fix: recolor static counts to blue (data) or emerald (completed/positive); mint only on actual click targets. Do as a repo-wide grep for text-accent-brand / var(--teal) on non-interactive text — complements the existing report-style-drift.ts baseline. Highest-leverage, lowest-risk item. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome follow-on — tame the up-to-nine equal hero StatCards
items.notes: WorkspaceHome (WH-F1) renders up to nine StatCards all at size=hero (Site Health, Search Clicks, Users, Rank Changes, Traffic Value, Content Decay, Content Pipeline, Content Velocity, Coverage Gaps) — a sharper version of CC-F2 where nine hero metrics means no hero. Fix: choose 2-3 hero metrics, demote the rest to a compact size=default secondary rail (still present, still deep-linked). Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — collapse four health representations into one canonical block
items.notes: Site Health appears four times on one screen: WorkspaceHealthBadge (under header), the Site Health StatCard, the MetricRing trailing inside that card, and WorkspaceHealthBar. One number, four widgets. Fix: keep the StatCard + trailing MetricRing as the single canonical health block; drop the standalone badge; fold HealthBar's setup% into the onboarding consolidation (design-wh-setup-triplicated). Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — recolor Users metric off purple; tokenize hardcoded iconColors
items.notes: The Users StatCard sets iconColor='#a78bfa' (purple) — a direct violation of the fourth color law (purple = admin AI only, never client-facing data). Several sibling cards also hardcode hex (#22d3ee, #4ade80, #f87171) instead of tokens. Fix: recolor Users to var(--blue) (it's a data metric); replace every hardcoded hex iconColor with a token so light mode + palette shifts stay correct; keep purple exclusive to AdminChat/admin intelligence. Pairs with the repo-wide color sweep. Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: Color sweep — tokenize repo-wide hardcoded hex iconColors/SVG/inline colors to CHART_SERIES_COLORS/tokens
items.notes: ~50+ hardcoded hex instances beyond the 3 Law-4 purple cards (RedirectManager, SiteArchitecture, LocalSeoVisibilityPanel, GbpAuthenticatedReviewsPanel, OutcomeScorecard, matrix/CellDetailPanel, client charts, audit/*, etc.) map to existing CHART_SERIES_COLORS constants or tokens. Keep brand-justified (#059669 OpenAI, #ea580c Anthropic, chart-hex-ok) and CSS-var fallbacks. Add missing constants to src/components/ui/constants.ts. Wave 0b. See docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-30-design-cleanup-audit.md §1b. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — show setup tasks in one place, not three
items.notes: Link Webflow / connect GSC / connect GA4 appears simultaneously in the OnboardingChecklist (top), WorkspaceHealthBar recommendations, and Needs Attention 'setup suggestions'. Same three tasks rendered three times for a half-configured workspace. Fix: give setup tasks one owner (OnboardingChecklist while unconfigured); once dismissed/complete, HealthBar + Needs Attention surface operational follow-ups only. Never render the same connect-task in two components at once. Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — extract one shared NeedsAttention/AttentionRow component
items.notes: Command Center and Workspace Home both render a 'Needs Attention' worklist but with different layouts: Command Center uses dot + Badge value + workspace name + chevron; Workspace Home uses colored icon + label + sub + ArrowUpRight plus a collapsible 'setup suggestions' sub-group. Same job, two visual languages. Fix: extract one AttentionRow / NeedsAttention component into the flow/ group with a single severity-color mapping and interaction; both screens consume it. Closes the divergence for good. Artifacts: ux-review-command-center.html (F3), ux-review-workspace-home.html (F5). [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — lead with the worklist; consider tabbing the lower sections
items.notes: ~10 stacked full-width sections; the most actionable block (Needs Attention) lands below nine metric cards + the health bar, well under the fold. Fix: order as worklist (after onboarding for new workspaces) -> trimmed metric grid -> operational sections; consider grouping the lower half under the existing TabBar (Overview/Pipeline/Activity) so the default view isn't a ten-section scroll. Depends on design-wh-statcard-hierarchy + design-x-needs-attention-shared. Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: The Issue (client) — bring the Content Plan up directly under the verdict
items.notes: On the spine branch the Content Plan (the only retainer action, 'Request this') sits at slot 5, beneath three stacked proof surfaces (verdict $, outcome count, money/ROI frame) plus the loop footer. A client convinced by the verdict may never scroll to act. Fix: keep verdict as the lead, then surface Content Plan directly under it (verdict -> plan -> proof), with outcome count + money + wins following. Strong A/B candidate on request-rate. Note: the flag-OFF branch already leads with the plan. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: The Issue (client) — render ROIDashboard once, not twice
items.notes: Slot 3 mounts <ROIDashboard compact /> and 'Under the hood' mounts <ROIDashboard compact={false} /> again — the same dollar story twice on one page (possible double fetch, 'which number is right?' confusion). Fix: render ROI once; keep the compact money frame at slot 3 and let 'Under the hood' hold only methodology/tables, or make the compact frame expand in place. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: The Issue (client) — relate the top 'Your turn' strip to the 'Ask your strategist' loop
items.notes: ActionQueueStrip (slot 0, 'Your turn') and IssueLoopFooter (slot 4, 'the full pending-decisions queue/loop') are two pending-work surfaces bookending the page; a client may read the footer as new work. Fix: make the top strip the count/jump ('3 decisions waiting v') that anchors to the footer detail, sharing one visual language. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — define one section-header treatment in the design system
items.notes: On The Issue, a top-level section header is variously a muted h2.t-label kicker, a SectionCard title bar, or a <summary> row — three weights for one job, making a long scroll hard to skim. Fix: standardize one section-header treatment (the t-label kicker) for top-level sections and reserve SectionCard headers for cards within a section. Applies app-wide. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Closeout: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — unify the WorkflowStepper and tab bar into one journey
items.notes: Two navigations describe the same flow differently: the WorkflowStepper says Strategy->Briefs->Posts->Publish while the tab bar says Planner/Calendar/Briefs/Posts/Subscriptions. 'Strategy' leaves the page, 'Publish' and 'Subscriptions' are the same destination under two names, and Planner/Calendar exist only in tabs. Fix: align stepper phases 1:1 with tabs and labels (rename Subscriptions->Publish; point stepper 'Strategy' at the Planner tab, not off-page); treat Calendar as a view toggle, not a peer phase. Stepper shows where you are; tabs are how you move. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — replace the hand-rolled tab bar with the TabBar primitive
items.notes: The sub-tab bar is built inline from <Button variant='ghost'> with a manual border-b-2 mint underline — re-implementing the TabBar component the system ships and WorkspaceHome already uses. They will drift in padding, hit-target, and keyboard nav. Fix: adopt the TabBar primitive (supports icons + mint active state + arrow-key nav + 44px hit target); move Export beside it as an action, not a fake tab. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — roll up four pre-content alert bands
items.notes: Between the stepper and tab content sit four full-width bands: health summary, content-decay alert, cannibalization warnings, AI-suggested briefs — three of them amber/red, so the page reads as alarmed before any action. Fix: keep the neutral health summary as the one persistent bar; roll decay + cannibalization + suggestions into a single collapsible 'Alerts & suggestions (N)' strip, or move each into the tab it acts on (decay->Posts, suggestions->Briefs). Reach the work in one screen. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — neutralize mint/amber on static counts in the health bar
items.notes: The health summary renders brief/matrix counts in text-accent-brand (mint) and the posts count in text-accent-warning (amber) — static data wearing action/warning colors (same drift as CC-F5; amber implies a problem that isn't there). Fix: counts are data -> neutral bright (or blue); reserve amber for genuine decay/cannibalization warnings. Folds into the repo-wide color-law sweep (design-color-law-sweep-mint-on-data). Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — add a Menu/Dropdown primitive to the design system
items.notes: The Content Pipeline Export control hand-rolls its own open state, click-outside handler, positioned panel, and per-row CSV/JSON buttons — and the design system has no Dropdown/Menu primitive, so the next menu gets hand-rolled differently. This is the first genuine NEW-component need surfaced by the review program. Fix: add a Menu/Dropdown primitive (trigger + positioned panel + click-outside + keyboard dismiss) and adopt it here. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: The Issue cockpit — keep Send issue in reach of the staging queue
items.notes: Send issue lives in IssueHeader at the top (canSend = stagedCount > 0), but the operator builds the staged set in the BackingMovesQueue near the bottom of the spine. Workflow becomes stage-at-bottom -> scroll-up-to-send, with the consuming button off-screen while staging. Fix: add a sticky send bar (or a Send affordance docked to BackingMovesQueue) that appears once stagedCount > 0, showing the staged count + Send from where staging happens; keep the header Send too. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — define one Disclosure/collapsible-section pattern; split the catch-all drawers
items.source: UX review 2026-06-30 · AC-F2 (mirrors client 'Under the hood')
items.notes: The cockpit's 'Supporting detail' <details> holds eight unrelated surfaces (leads, keyword-targets link, content-work-order lens, trust ladder, orient, content gaps, cannibalization, strategy diff) behind one all-or-nothing toggle. The client TheIssueClientPage 'Under the hood' does the same (ring + full ROI + leads + competitors). Fix: split each into 2-3 labeled disclosures grouped by job, AND define a single Disclosure/collapsible-section pattern in the design system (consistent summary chrome + behavior + sub-grouping) consumed by both screens. Retires AC-F2 and the client under-the-hood catch-all. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: The Issue cockpit — tokenize the POV-staleness nudge (raw amber)
items.notes: The 'POV may be out of date' banner uses raw Tailwind text-amber-400 / border-amber-500/30 / bg-amber-500/5 instead of the --amber token + the soft-warning surface convention (StatusBadge/InlineBanner). Won't follow palette/light-theme changes; a second way of spelling 'warning surface'. Fix: use the warning tokens + shared soft-amber convention. Pairs with WH-F3 / the color-law + tokenize-hardcoded-hex work. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: The Issue cockpit — dock 'Add a recommendation' to the BackingMovesQueue header
items.notes: A right-aligned 'Add a recommendation' button sits on its own line between the Drafted POV editor and the BackingMovesQueue — an orphan action floating in the gap. It feeds the queue, so it should live in the queue's header action slot (the SectionCard action pattern). Removes the orphan line and puts the control on the object it modifies. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: Client shell — finish the two-speed nav migration; retire the 12-tab legacy nav
items.notes: Behind client-ia-v2 the nav collapses from ~12 top-level tabs (Overview, Performance, Health, Strategy, Inbox, Content Plan, Plans, ROI, Brand, Results, Deep Dive, Settings) to four. Twelve is far too many for a client; while the flag is mid-rollout the product has two front doors depending on flag state. Fix: commit to the two-speed shell and retire legacy nav — Overview = verdict; Deep Dive holds Performance/Health/Rankings/Content sub-views; Inbox = loop; Settings absorbs Plans/Brand. Highest-impact move on this screen. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — define each tab panel once; collapse duplicate mounts
items.notes: Health, Performance, Strategy, Content Plan, Brand, and Plans each render both as a standalone tab AND as a slot inside Deep Dive/Settings, with the full prop list duplicated inline (HealthTab + actionPlanSlot repeated verbatim). Two routes reach identical content and can silently drift. Fix: define each panel once (a const or a registry keyed by tab id) and reference it wherever it appears; under the two-speed shell give each surface a single home (Health/Performance/Strategy live inside Deep Dive, not also top-level). Depends on design-cd-finish-ia-v2-nav. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — one prioritized notice region above tabs
items.notes: Above tab content sit up to three stacked notices: trial-countdown (or trial-ended) banner, a section-errors InlineBanner, and a per-tab SeoEducationTip — the same 'wall before the work' as CP-F3. Fix: one notice region with priority order (errors > trial > education tip) showing at most one at a time / docked compactly; the education tip yields to a real error. Reuse the CP-F3 consolidation pattern. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — stop echoing the nav label as the per-tab page title
items.notes: Every tab renders a PageHeader whose title is just the active tab's label (click Health -> page says Health); the nav already shows location, so the H1 echoes it without adding info — only the subtitle carries something new. Fix: drop the redundant title echo (let subtitle + content carry the page) or make the header earn its space (verdict line, freshness stamp, the tab's primary action). Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — route trial banners through InlineBanner / warning tokens
items.notes: Trial-countdown and trial-ended banners use raw bg-amber-500/8 border-amber-500/20 and bg-red-500/8 border-red-500/20 mixed with text-accent-warning/danger token classes — a half-tokenized warning surface and a third spelling of 'amber notice' alongside InlineBanner and the cockpit staleness nudge (AC-F3). Fix: route both through InlineBanner (or its token-backed warning/danger variants) so every alert surface shares one definition. Folds into the tokenize-hardcoded-hex theme. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — promote the tinted-gradient card to a StatCard/card tone prop
items.notes: The 'tinted gradient + matching border' card treatment is hand-rolled with slightly different opacities per surface: the verdict shell uses from-teal-500/8 border-teal-500/15; the outcome StatCards pass className='bg-gradient-to-br from-emerald-500/10 border-emerald-500/20'. Fix: promote it to a first-class StatCard/card tone prop (tone='emerald' -> gradient + border baked in at one canonical opacity) so the verdict hero and outcome band share one definition and future surfaces inherit it. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Closeout: shipped]

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Brand Intelligence Slice — unify voice + identity (P1–P5)
sprints.hours: TBD — phased, one PR per phase, staging-first
sprints.rationale: Surface a single authority-correct brand context (voice + approved identity) through the workspace intelligence facade so AI/recommendation consumers stop re-deriving it. P1 ships the read-only, behavior-neutral slice (registration + assembler + read-model extraction) behind the zero circular-dependency ratchet; P2 wires brand into the MCP content path (prepare_*_context) so agents get full voice DNA + identity — NOT a formatter/baseSlices change (server-side gen already gets full voice via buildSystemPrompt; contract #4). Plans: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-29-brand-slice-p1.md, docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-29-brand-slice-p2.md. Design: docs/designs/2026-06-26-brand-intelligence-slice.md.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P1 — read-only, behavior-neutral registration + assembler
items.notes: Registered the `brand` slice (BrandSlice interface, INTELLIGENCE_SLICES entry, WorkspaceIntelligence.brand?, registry entry) and assembler server/intelligence/brand-slice.ts. Voice read via the unchanged buildEffectiveBrandVoiceBlock (seo-context-source.ts); identity read via a newly-extracted leaf read-model server/brand-deliverable-read-model.ts (brand-identity.ts re-exports listDeliverables/getDeliverable for back-compat). Behavior-neutral: NOT added to PROMPT_FORMATTABLE_INTELLIGENCE_SLICES, no formatters.ts case, in no baseSlices — exactly like generationQuality. Cycle-kill boundary test guards the leaf-import contract; circular-deps stays server 0/0. P2 (prompt wiring) is the next phase.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P2 — wire brand into MCP prepare_*_context (voice DNA + identity to agents)

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P3 — get_brand_identity MCP read tool (identity + voice status, opt-in deliverables)

Input schema getBrandIdentityInputSchema mirrors getWorkspaceIntelligenceInputSchema's camelCase workspaceId. Lockstep registration in all three spots: server.ts ALL_TOOLS, server.ts dispatch branch, and the contract test's ALL_TOOLS array (plus the mcp-server-routing test's mocked tool set). TDD: failing unit test in mcp-tools-read-models.test.ts first (default no deliverables; includeDeliverables returns draft+approved; missing workspace/invalid args/assembly-throw → isError; no voice leakage), then implementation. circular-deps server 0/0. items.title: Brand intelligence slice P4 (Option A) — lock voice-block slice-parity invariant

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — 🔑 Keyword Hub Health — First Paint, Memory, and Universe Read-Path Hardening
sprints.rationale: The Keyword Hub/Keyword Universe crash pattern was most likely not caused directly by circular imports; it was caused by heavy keyword/local/intelligence read paths sharing one hot graph under memory pressure. The current Hub is much safer than the original path: rows use a skinny page-bounded read path, local SEO refreshes run sequentially with heap headroom, rank evidence is capped, junk gates exist, and large local-candidate evaluation remains hard-capped. This sprint turns those patch-level protections into an explicit performance architecture: measure first, remove duplicated first-paint reads, move the remaining heavy local_candidates exception toward the skinny model, fix known facet drift, and add regression guardrails so a future universe expansion cannot quietly re-open the OOM/page-crash class.
sprints.notes: Audit anchors (2026-06-26): `src/components/KeywordHub.tsx` fetched `/summary` and `/rows` separately on first paint; `server/domains/keyword-command-center/summary-service.ts` and `rows-service.ts` duplicated source loading across strategy, page keywords, tracked keywords, latest ranks, feedback, lost visibility, and local visibility; `filter=local_candidates` dispatched through `buildKeywordCommandCenterRowsViaModel()` / `buildKeywordCommandCenterModel()` and required an explicit OOM cap; `summary-service.ts` hardcoded `notChecked: 0`; `candidate-boundary.ts` appeared to have a duplicated secondary-keyword junk check. DONE 2026-06-27: first paint uses the combined initial view, shared source snapshots back summary/rows/detail/initial, local_candidates uses a capped skinny projection instead of the full model, the dead Not Checked facet is no longer exposed, and the secondary junk check was re-audited as already gone. Success metric: Keyword Hub first paint remains stable on large workspaces while `npm run check:circular-deps`, focused KCC tests, and an explicit perf/heap smoke keep the read path inside known limits. Do not change keyword scoring semantics or lifecycle behavior in this sprint.
